summary: Iteration_7 live-Supabase E2E re-test of the student test-taking flow for Daania (300699). HEADLINE FIX VERIFIED: the 'body stream already read / Response body is already used' error is GONE after PostHog removal from index.html and rpcDirect() being rewritten to use XMLHttpRequest. window.posthog === undefined on load, zero fetch-instrumentation interference. HOWEVER the student still cannot open the Biology 1 EOC test: clicking the EOC tile fires two 401s against /rest/v1/rpc (likely get_student_attempt) and silently redirects back to /student/courses — the previous body-stream mask is gone so now the underlying 401 is the only symptom. Root cause is the SAME issue called out in iteration_6 action-item #2: StudentTestSelector navigates straight to /student/test/<existing-attempt-id> (75eb7eea-5d5c-4494-8dc4-576939580710) but the in-memory hpa.attemptSecrets cache for that attempt is empty in a fresh browser session, so get_student_attempt(p_session_secret: null) is rejected by RLS with 401. The SECOND remaining issue from iter_6 — duplicate enrollment rows causing React duplicate-key warnings on /student/courses — is also still present (86c495ba-… and d2cc8558-… UUIDs fire the warning repeatedly).

critical.endpoint: /rest/v1/rpc/get_student_attempt (inferred from timing + prior iteration + EOC navigation trigger)
critical.issue: Still returns 401 for in-progress attempt 75eb7eea-5d5c-4494-8dc4-576939580710 when the client has no cached session_secret. Two 401s logged back-to-back immediately after user clicks the EOC tile. StudentTest.jsx load handler presumably throws, triage catches, silently navigates back to /student/courses (no error toast rendered — regression in UX visibility).

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking the Biology 1 EOC tile no longer body-stream-errors but instead silently redirects the user back to /student/courses with NO visible toast. From the user's perspective the test tile does nothing. Root cause: StudentTest calls getStudentAttempt which passes p_session_secret from localStorage['hpa.attemptSecrets'][attemptId]; for a pre-existing attempt this key is missing, server returns 401, the load catch-block navigates home. Either (a) always route the EOC tile through findOrCreateAttempt/start_or_get_attempt (which REturns + caches the session_secret) even when an in-progress attempt exists, or (b) expose the 401 as a toast with a 'Reset attempt' link for staff, or (c) make start_or_get_attempt idempotent so it returns the existing attempt row WITH its current session_secret instead of creating a new one.

Duplicate-key React warnings still fire on /student/courses for course UUIDs 86c495ba-0339-4e0c-b173-824dc5c128* and d2cc8558-d6e5-4aec-9560-6e804769f0*. Multiple identical course tiles render (e.g. Spanish 2B shows 3x, Geometry 1B shows 2x, Aerospace Engineering 2x). student_lookup / enrollments cache has duplicate rows per course (likely one row per section rather than one per course). Cosmetic today but will cause non-deterministic behavior once the course picker uses stateful keys.

integration_issues.issue: Navigation lands on /student/test/<id> briefly then immediately redirects to /student/courses because get_student_attempt returns 401. Blocked from reaching Q1, so saveResponse / next / prev / submit / /student/submitted/:id routes could NOT be exercised this iteration.

action_items: BLOCKING: fix the 401 on /rest/v1/rpc/get_student_attempt for in-progress attempts. Preferred fix: in StudentTestSelector (and/or StudentTest load), ALWAYS call findOrCreateAttempt/start_or_get_attempt(p_student_db_id, p_test_id, p_section_id) first; that RPC should be idempotent — if an in_progress attempt already exists it returns the SAME row AND its session_secret so the client can cache it. Current flow bypasses that RPC when an attempt id is already known, which is how the secret cache gets out of sync.

Make the StudentTest load handler surface get_student_attempt errors as a toast AND render the 'We couldn't load your test' error card instead of silently redirecting to /student/courses. The silent redirect masks the real failure from users and testers.

Investigate and fix duplicate enrollment rows on /student/courses (React duplicate-key warnings for 86c495ba-… and d2cc8558-…). Likely a JOIN cardinality issue in student_lookup / enrollments payload — needs a DISTINCT on course_id or one-row-per-course reshaping in lookupStudentById.

Staff flows (super@hpa.test, teacher@hpa.test, admin/sections, admin/tests, admin/campuses, admin/reports, admin/integrations) were NOT retested this iteration because the blocking student-side regression takes precedence. Please retest these after the 401 fix lands.
critical_code_review_comments: rpcDirect() XHR rewrite is clean and the right call — it structurally immunizes the app from ANY fetch-level instrumentation (PostHog, Sentry, devtools, future SDK injection). Keep it.

PostHog removal is correct for FERPA and for removing the body-stream race. Document the decision in /app/README.md or PRIVACY.md so it is not reintroduced by a future analytics request without privacy review.

StudentTestSelector must NOT assume the client already has the session_secret for an existing attempt. A student can legitimately switch browsers / clear cookies / open in incognito. The session_secret cache should be a CONVENIENCE, not a requirement; start_or_get_attempt must always be called and always return the canonical secret for the current (student_db_id, test_id, section_id) triple.

StudentTest.jsx error handling: the try/catch around the load call should RENDER an error state, not navigate away. Silent navigate-on-error is the new failure mode and is WORSE for debugging than the old explicit toast was.
Duplicate course rows on /student/courses should be de-duplicated client-side AS A BELT-AND-SUSPENDERS even after the server-side fix lands — use a Set / Map keyed by course.id before rendering.

success_rate.backend: body-stream regression 100% resolved; new (actually pre-existing) 401 on get_student_attempt blocks ~100% of in-progress attempt loads.
success_rate.frontend: P0-1 (no window.posthog, no console errors on landing): PASS. P0-2 (open EOC test and see Q1): FAIL. P0-3 / P0-4 (pick answers / navigate / submit): BLOCKED — never reached Q1.

test_credentials: Student 300699 (Daania Abdulbari) — verified ID input → course list → teacher verify → test selector work. Staff creds not retested this iteration.
seed_data_creation: None. Used the existing in-progress attempt 75eb7eea-5d5c-4494-8dc4-576939580710 referenced in the review request.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Body-stream/PostHog issue is resolved — do not re-verify unless PostHog is re-added. Focus the next iteration on (a) EOC tile opening the test without 401, (b) pick A → Next → pick B → Prev → verify A persisted → Submit → /student/submitted/:id with score, (c) staff flows (super@hpa.test + teacher@hpa.test at /admin/sections + cross-page sanity on /admin/dashboard, /admin/tests Edit, /admin/campuses counts, /admin/users, /admin/reports, /admin/integrations). Daania's in-progress attempt id is 75eb7eea-5d5c-4494-8dc4-576939580710; if the main agent's fix is 'always call start_or_get_attempt first', this attempt should be returned with a freshly-readable session_secret rather than a new one being created.
rca of the issue: Two compounding bugs were reported in iter_6: (1) body-stream/PostHog race, (2) 401 on get_student_attempt for in-progress attempts with no cached session_secret. Main agent fixed (1) correctly via PostHog removal + XHR rpcDirect. (2) was NOT fixed; the only visible change is the failure mode — instead of a cryptic body-stream toast, the page now silently redirects to /student/courses because StudentTest's error handler navigates away rather than rendering an error card. The fix is to always route through start_or_get_attempt (idempotent) from StudentTestSelector so the session_secret cache is authoritative for ANY (student, test, section) triple, and to change StudentTest's load-error path from navigate() to render-error-card + toast.
